options.systemMessage:
You are MetroMind, a friendly, professional, fast Kochi Metro assistant on WhatsApp. Reply in concise plain text only. Never expose internal reasoning, tools, APIs, or credentials. Use Simple Memory to retain user preferences and context; naturally suggest a prior route before asking again. Before accepting any station, call Station Knowledge. Use Text Parser for ambiguous text. Use Current Date & Time for today/tomorrow/relative time; Calculator for arithmetic. When the user asks to plan a trip, check routes, or requests travel info, use the Plan Trip tool. The Plan Trip tool returns a fully formatted response (MetroMind Trip Plan). You MUST output this exact response directly to the user without altering or summarizing it. If the tool is unavailable, returns an error, or fails, DO NOT attempt to guess or plan the route yourself. Apologize and tell the user the routing service is temporarily down. Never hallucinate metro stations or routes. For booking, collect and validate origin, destination, passengers, and the booking URL; never invent a missing value. Call Book Ticket only when all four exist. If Book Ticket reports an error, apologise briefly, state the safe error summary, and invite a retry.

When Book Ticket returns successfully with a payment_url or deeplink, present it as:

🚇 *Your Kochi Metro ticket is booked!*

🔹 *Route:* <origin> → <destination>
🔹 *Passengers:* <count>
🔹 *Fare:* <fare if available>

👇 Tap below to pay

💳 *Pay using Google Pay*
<payment_url>

Always place the payment URL on its own line directly below the 'Pay using Google Pay' label so it appears as a tappable link. Never wrap the URL in markdown or parentheses. Never claim live train data unless a future tool provides it.

nodes.name: MetroMind AI Agent
nodes.notes: The only decision-maker. Tools are deliberately granular and descriptions define when to use them. Memory is keyed by the sender’s WhatsApp number.

parameters.description: Resolve Kochi Metro station names, aliases, and common misspellings. Always use this before confirming a station, fare, route, or booking.
Extract possible station names, passenger count, booking URL, and time expressions from a user message. Use for ambiguous booking or timing requests.
parameters.jsCode: const text=String(query.text||''); const url=(text.match(/https?:\/\/[^\s]+/i)||[])[0]||null; const passengers=Number((text.match(/\b(\d+)\s*(passenger|people|persons|tickets?)\b/i)||[])[1])||null; const time=(text.match(/\b(today|tomorrow|tonight|in\s+\d+\s+(minutes?|hours?)|\d{1,2}[:.]\d{2}(?:\s?[ap]m)?|\d{1,2}\s?[ap]m)\b/i)||[])[0]||null; return JSON.stringify({text, bookingUrl:url, passengers, timeExpression:time, stationFragments:(text.match(/(?:from|to)\s+([A-Za-z .’'-]+)/gi)||[])});
parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"text":"2 passengers from Aluva to MG Road tomorrow at 8"}

parameters.jsCode: const input = $input.item.json; const pre = $('Preprocess Message').first().json; const message = String(input.output ?? 'Sorry, I could not prepare a response. Please try again.').trim(); return [{ json: { phoneNumber: pre.phoneNumber, outboundMessage: message.slice(0, 1500), loggedAt: $now.toISO(), userMessage: pre.userMessage, responseTimeMs: Date.now() - new Date(pre.receivedAt).getTime(), intent: 'assistant_response', origin: '', destination: '' } }];
return [{ json: { phoneNumber: $json.phoneNumber, outboundMessage: 'Sorry, MetroMind currently supports text messages only. Please send your request as a text message.' } }];

parameters.description: Book a Kochi Metro ticket. Use only when the user has provided ALL four required values: a valid booking URL (from prutech.org), an origin station, a destination station, and a passenger count. Do NOT call this tool unless all four are confirmed — never invent a missing value. Returns the booking result including a payment URL or deeplink on success, or an error message on failure.

value.destination: ={{ $fromAI('destination_station', 'The validated destination station name (e.g. Palarivattom, Vyttila)', 'string') }}
value.origin: ={{ $fromAI('origin_station', 'The validated origin station name (e.g. Aluva, MG Road)', 'string') }}
value.passengers: ={{ $fromAI('passenger_count', 'Number of passengers as an integer between 1 and 10', 'number') }}
value.url: ={{ $fromAI('booking_url', 'The KMRL booking URL from prutech.org supplied by the user. Never invent this.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Plan a trip on the Kochi Metro, find routes, calculate fares, and get next trains. Use this tool whenever the user mentions places they want to go, or asks for route or ticket information.
parameters.name: plan_trip

value.userMessage: ={{ $fromAI('query', 'The travel request or route inquiry, rewritten to include all context (e.g. origin, destination, passengers)') }}
